title: Junior Software Engineer
description:
Millions of customers pass through Dollarama every day in search of possibility, affordability, and items they did not know they needed until they saw them. At Dollarama, we are on a mission to redefine accessible consumption by transforming everyday transactions into meaningful moments of perceived value.

As part of our engineering team, you will help power systems that enable customers to discover, evaluate, and ultimately purchase products that may or may not meaningfully improve their lives, depending on how effectively we communicate their importance.

Here, innovation means scaling low-cost retail to high-impact digital experiences, where real-time shelf simulation, global discount logistics, and AI-driven inventory intuition come together to create a seamless journey from curiosity to checkout.

We believe in building with purpose, even when that purpose is continuously evolving. You will work in an environment where ambiguity fuels creativity, requirements are living conversations, and every deployment is an opportunity to redefine what was previously considered finished.

At Dollarama, technology is more than infrastructure. It is a storytelling engine that shapes perception, drives engagement, and unlocks new ways to deliver value at scale.

Join us in creating systems that empower millions to make confident purchasing decisions, even when those decisions are, at their core, entirely optional and a reflection of our success in shaping perceived need.
responsibilities: Nod confidently when nothing makes sense
Use corporate jargon and buzzwords during weekly mandatory meetings to sound productive
Train AI on customer disappointment, then use it to forecast future disappointment
Translate unclear executive decisions into actionable confusion for engineering teams
Scroll on TikTok while waiting for Claude Code to finish its tasks
Collaborate with AI systems that observe your work in order to eventually replicate your ability to tolerate it
Generate status updates that imply progress without requiring actual progress to occur
Take ownership of systems, failures, and decisions made before your employment
Ensure 99.9999% uptime for systems that are intentionally unstable

basic: Ability to  least one programming language and a willingness to immediately abandon it
Ability to work in environments where production outages are considered learning opportunities
Fluent in multiple programming languages; prepared to learn a new one and abandon it by Monday
At least 20+ years experience with React, CI/CD pipelines, PyTorch, and NumPy
Proficiency in Kubernetes, watercolor painting, and emergency crisis diplomacy
Have made atleast 500 contributions to major open-source projects
Background in competitive eSports and 19th-century French economic theory
preferred: Experience with cloud platforms that scale elastically in response to unknown demand spikes
At least 30+ years of experience with C++, Java, Python, Rust, Assembly, GoLand, Ruby, and SQL
A thorough understanding of Romantic literature and the history of Baroque art
Minimum 400+ words per minute typing speed while skydiving
50+ years of experience with of JavaScript, CSS, DOM, HTTP protocols
Familiarity with frameworks announced, unannounced, then rebranded mid-cycle
Track record of improving latency by reducing user expectations rather than improving performance
Ability to translate ancient philosophical texts into production-ready API documentation

title: Software Development Intern
description:
At FreshCo, we are thrilled, humbled, and deeply excited to announce an exciting opportunity for an entry-level intern role that requires the experience, resilience, and emotional stability of a senior engineer with 10+ years in the field.

As part of our team, you will work on mission-critical systems that power millions of customer experiences worldwide, while also occasionally updating button colors and writing documentation that will never be read again.

We are a family. This means we support each other, grow together, and occasionally undergo restructuring together. Please note that family membership may be revoked at any time based on quarterly performance metrics.

In this role, you will take full ownership of your work, your team’s work, and problems that existed long before you joined. You will be expected to deliver high-quality, production-ready code in an environment where timelines are ambitious, requirements are fluid, and expectations are described as ‘flexible.’

You will also have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge AI tools, which may or may not eventually replace parts of your workflow. Familiarity with these tools is required, as you will be evaluated on your ability to both use and compete with them.

Applicants should demonstrate a strong passion for being underpaid relative to market value, paired with a willingness to describe this as ‘an incredible learning opportunity.’

As part of the application process, you may be asked to complete several short assessments, personality evaluations, and captcha verifications to confirm that you are, in fact, human. Please note that failure to correctly identify traffic lights, bicycles, or slightly distorted letters may result in immediate disqualification.

We encourage candidates to bring their authentic selves to work, provided those selves align with our core values, communication style, and brand guidelines.

Join us in building the future of technology, one sprint at a time, and experience the excitement of seeing your code deployed to production shortly before being refactored by someone else. Apply today to take the first step in a rewarding journey where you will grow, learn, and occasionally wonder if the robots you are helping build are quietly preparing to take your place.
responsibilities: Collaborate with teams and former execs to ship features that will be rewritten within weeks
Demonstrate passion, resilience, and gratitude for the opportunity to be underpaid
Write production code that balances performance, legacy debt, and undocumented code from 2007
Deliver fast, high-quality work with minimal time, context, or support
Take full ownership of projects while requiring approval for every decision
Fix critical outages immediately, preferably before they occur
Use AI tools to increase productivity while competing with them for long-term employment viability
Show passion for growth opportunities (compensation not included)
Be on-call 24/7 to ensure work-life balance
Ship high-impact features on tight deadlines, then immediately deprecate them

basic: Master's and PhD in Computer Science, Theoretical Mathematics, and Corporate Wishful Thinking
30 years of experience in C++, Java, and Python
Must have at least 3 wins in the Canadian Computing Competition
Proven ability to design systems that are both highly complex and immediately understandable
preferred: Previous experience as a CEO, CTO, or founding engineer at a major  company such as Apple
Experience managing large-scale, high-risk systems
Deep familiarity with AWS, GCP, Azure, and any frameworks that have not yet been documented
Working knowledge of robotics, machine learning, and one field that doesn't exist yet
Exceptional ability to confidently use corporate jargon and buzzwords to avoid responsibility
Demonstrated ability to learn and master new technologies instantly

title: Glass Cleaner
description:
Amazon is seeking a Glass Cleaning Intern to help maintain the visual illusion that everything is under control.

In this role, you will be responsible for restoring transparency to surfaces that immediately lose it, often within seconds. Tasks include removing fingerprints, questioning your standards, and re-cleaning areas that were already clean but are now, apparently, not.

As part of our team, you will work on mission-critical glass infrastructures that power millions of customer reflections worldwide, while also occasionally wiping the same window multiple times because someone walked past it and breathed. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams across multiple time zones, ensuring that glass remains transparent regardless of when it becomes dirty again.

You will operate in a fast-paced environment where definitions of “spotless” shift depending on lighting, management perspective, and who is currently looking at the glass.

You should possess the instincts of someone who has spent a decade chasing invisible streaks and losing arguments with sunlight.

You will also have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge AI-assisted cleaning systems, which may or may not eventually replace your ability to clean glass. Familiarity with these tools is required, as you will be evaluated on your ability to both use them and outperform them.

Compensation is competitive in the sense that it exists.

Successful candidates are detail-oriented, resilient, and capable of accepting that perfection is both expected and temporary.

Apply today to take the first step in a rewarding journey where you will grow, learn, and occasionally wonder if the automated cleaning systems you are helping train are quietly preparing to achieve a higher standard of cleanliness than you ever could.
responsibilities: Ensure all glass surfaces meet enterprise-grade clarity standards
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align on wiping strategy and long-term smudge vision
Own the full lifecycle of each window, from initial cleaning to inevitable re-dirtying
Be on-call for high-priority glass incidents, including fingerprints, rain, and executive walkthroughs
Proactively identify issues before they are visible, and resolve them after escalation
Architect streak-free solutions at scale while preserving the original intent of each smudge
Monitor glass performance metrics in real time (clarity, reflectivity, executive satisfaction)
Align cleaning strategy with quarterly vision, long-term roadmap, and weather conditions
Work alongside automation tools that are being trained using your own technique

basic: 10+ years of hands-on glass cleaning experience in fast-paced, high-stakes environments
Advanced degree in a related field (Cleaning, Optics, or Unpaid Labor Studies preferred)
Proven ability to remain standing for extended periods
Ability to distinguish between 'clean,' 'almost clean,' and 'clean enough for leadership'
preferred: Prior experience cleaning glass at scale (skyscrapers, data centers, etc)
Familiarity with industry-standard tools and experimental techniques not yet proven to work
Experience working with or being replaced by automated cleaning systems
Proven track record of staying motivated despite immediate and recurring undoing of your work
Demonstrated ability to learn and master new cleaning techniques instantly
Ability to interpret vague feedback such as “it just feels off” and act decisively
